470	
471	\begin{lemma}\leanok
472	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
473	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
474	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
475	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
476	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars,
477	        lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt,
478	        lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
479	% NOTE: the carrier-wall obstruction to `hloc` is resolved (restriction-of-scalars /
480	% scalar-tower instances feed lem:powers_restrictScalars; lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule
481	% supplies the R'-side localization). The single remaining obligation is the open-naturality of
482	% the section comparison e_{D(a)}, established as a prose remark in the proof of
483	% lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt; the proof sketch below derives each per-a hloc(a) from the top-level
484	% localization plus that naturality square for the inclusion D(a) ⊆ ⊤, so no section-level square
485	% is hand-proved per a.
486	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
487	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
488	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
489	  \[
490	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\,\widetilde{M}
491	    \;\cong\;
492	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)},
493	  \]
494	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
495	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
496	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
497	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) is the \(R\)-module obtained from \(M\) by
498	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). That is, pushing the quasi-coherent
499	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) forward along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to
500	  this canonical identification, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of \(M\).
501	
502	  This is the classical fact that the direct image of a quasi-coherent sheaf along
503	  a morphism of affine schemes is computed on modules by restriction of scalars; it
504	  is the affine companion of the pullback formula (the affine case of the
505	  pushforward analogue of Stacks ``$\widetilde{M}$ and pullback''). It is recorded
506	  here as bespoke project infrastructure because the affine-pushforward
507	  identification does not appear as a ready-made result in the existing
508	  quasi-coherent sheaf theory.
509	\end{lemma}
510	
511	\begin{proof}
512	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars, lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt, lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
513	  We build the isomorphism \emph{directly on a basis of basic opens}. This is the
514	  non-circular route: checking the comparison map on basic opens yields the object
515	  isomorphism, and quasi-coherence of the pushforward is then a corollary, not a
516	  prerequisite. (One must not reverse this order: the global-sections comparison
517	  \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{M}\bigr) \cong
518	  \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) of
519	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} alone would only upgrade to an object
520	  isomorphism once the pushforward is already known to be quasi-coherent, so
521	  quasi-coherence cannot be deduced from an object isomorphism built that way.)
522	
523	  \emph{The comparison morphism.} There is a canonical morphism of
524	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
525	  \[
526	    \alpha : \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)}
527	    \longrightarrow \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M},
528	  \]
529	  namely the tilde-adjoint of the global-sections identification of
530	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}. We show \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
531	
532	  \medskip
533	  \emph{Reduction to basic opens.} The basic opens \(D(a)\), for \(a \in R\), form a
534	  basis of the topology of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). By the basis-local criterion
535	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} it suffices to check that \(\alpha\)
536	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over each basic open \(D(a)\).
537	
538	  \medskip
539	  \emph{The computation over \(D(a)\).} On the source, the sections of
540	  \(\widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) over \(D(a)\) are the
541	  localization \(\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)[1/a]\) as an
542	  \(R[1/a]\)-module. On the target, since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) =
543	  D(\varphi a)\), the sections of \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*
544	  \widetilde{M}\) over \(D(a)\) are the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
545	  \(D(\varphi a)\), i.e.\ the localization \(M[1/\varphi a]\), regarded as an
546	  \(R[1/a]\)-module by restriction of scalars along the induced map
547	  \(R[1/a] \to R'[1/\varphi a]\). These two localizations agree: the element \(a\)
548	  acts on \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) through \(\varphi a\), so localizing
549	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at the multiplicative set generated by \(a\) is
550	  the same module as localizing \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by
551	  \(\varphi a\). Both are the universal localization of
552	  \(M\) inverting \(\varphi a\), so the comparison map \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism
553	  on the sections over \(D(a)\). As this holds for every basic open, \(\alpha\) is
554	  an isomorphism.
555	
556	  \medskip
557	  \emph{The explicit formalization route over \(D(a)\).} The agreement of the two
558	  localizations just described is the entire content, but it must be realized
559	  through the global ring \(R\), because the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
560	  sections over \(D(a)\) is built from the global-sections module of
561	  \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) rather than directly from
562	  \(R[1/a]\). The reduction therefore proceeds exactly as the conditional
563	  assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} prescribes: it
564	  suffices to supply, for each \(a \in R\), the hypothesis \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) that
565	  the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) exhibits
566	  \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at
567	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). The \(R\)-action on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) is the honest
568	  restriction-of-scalars action carried by the scalar tower \(R \to R' \to {-}\)
569	  determined by \(\varphi\); against that structure the ring-change converse
570	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applicable, so the carrier of the
571	  \(R\)-module is exactly the one to which the localization claim refers, and no
572	  recursively-defined or ad-hoc section-level scalar action intervenes.
573	  We obtain \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transporting the already-known \(R'\)-side
574	  localization (Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}), in
575	  element-free fashion, across the open-indexed section comparison of
576	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} \emph{using its naturality in the open}
577	  (the naturality remark in the proof of
578	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}). This proceeds in three
579	  movements.
580	
581	  \emph{(1) The \(D(a)\)-level linear equivalence.} This is the component at
582	  \(U = D(a)\) of the open-indexed family of
583	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the \(R\)-linear isomorphism
584	  \[
585	    e_{D(a)} : \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
586	      \;\cong\;
587	      \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr) ,
588	  \]
589	  using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
590	  definitional. No fresh \(D(a)\)-level construction is needed: this is precisely the
591	  open-indexed isomorphism already supplied by
592	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} evaluated at the basic open \(D(a)\).
593	
594	  \emph{(2) The naturality square for \(D(a) \subseteq \top\).} The compatibility
595	  that relates \(e_{D(a)}\) to the \(\top\)-level isomorphism
596	  \(e_\top\) (the global-sections comparison of
597	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) is precisely the open-naturality square of the
598	  family \(\{e_U\}_U\) (established in the proof of
599	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}), instantiated at the inclusion
600	  \(D(a) \subseteq \top\): the square
601	  \[
602	    \begin{array}{ccc}
603	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, \top\bigr)
604	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_\top\;}
605	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top)\bigr) \\[4pt]
606	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}}\big\downarrow & &
607	        \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}} \\[4pt]
608	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
609	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{D(a)}\;}
610	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr)
611	    \end{array}
612	  \]
613	  commutes, using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
614	  definitional. Because the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) is a natural isomorphism, this single
615	  square is available uniformly in \(a\); there is no per-\(a\) section-level identity
616	  to re-prove by hand. It intertwines the source restriction
617	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) on the pushforward with the (restriction-of-scalars
618	  reading of the) target restriction \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}\) on
619	  \(\widetilde M\).
620	
621	  \emph{(3) Discharging \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transport.} The target restriction
622	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)} : \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
623	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a)) =
624	  M[1/\varphi a]\) as the \(\mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)-localization of
625	  \(M = \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) over \(R'\); this is exactly
626	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}. Apply the ring-change converse
627	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} (with \(R\)-algebra \(A = R'\) and submonoid
628	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\), so that
629	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)) —
630	  its \(\operatorname{Algebra} R\, R'\) and scalar-tower hypotheses being supplied by
631	  the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\) — to read this same map as
632	  the \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization over \(R\). The commuting naturality square
633	  of movement (2) intertwines this target restriction with the source restriction
634	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)} : \Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) by way of the
635	  isomorphisms \(e_\top\) and \(e_{D(a)}\); transporting the localization property
636	  across that square — via the transport-along-an-equivalence principle for localized
637	  modules — yields that \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) is the
638	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) over \(R\), i.e.\
639	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\). No section-level computation specific to the individual \(a\)
640	  is performed: the only \(a\)-dependent input is the instantiation of the single
641	  natural-isomorphism square at \(D(a)\). Feeding the family
642	  \(\{\mathrm{hloc}(a)\}_{a \in R}\) to the conditional assembly
643	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} produces the unconditional
644	  object isomorphism \(\alpha\).
645	
646	  \medskip
647	  \emph{Naturality in the open drives the transport.} The comparison assembled
648	  here, \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)} \cong
649	  \widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\), is natural in \emph{both}
650	  arguments — in the module \(M\) and in the open \(U\). The naturality in the
651	  module is the usual functoriality; the naturality in the open is the naturality
652	  remark in the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the family
653	  \(\{e_U\}_U\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} commutes with the
654	  structure-sheaf restriction maps on both sides. It is precisely this
655	  open-naturality that drives the localization transport \emph{uniformly}: the
656	  per-\(a\) localization fact \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) follows from the \(\top\)-level
657	  localization Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule} together with that
658	  naturality square for the inclusion
659	  \(D(a) \hookrightarrow \top\), rather than from a section-level naturality square
660	  re-proved by hand for each individual \(a\). This is the single remaining
661	  obligation of the construction; the carrier of the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
662	  sections is the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\), against which
663	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} applies directly.
664	
665	  The \(R\)-module structure on the \(R'\)-side sections to which the ring-change
666	  converse Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applied is the honest
667	  restriction-of-scalars structure: \(R\) acts through \(\varphi\), i.e.\ via the
668	  \(R\)-algebra structure on \(R'\) determined by \(\varphi\) and the associated
669	  scalar tower \(R \to R' \to M[1/\varphi a]\). It is \emph{not} an ad-hoc,
670	  recursively-defined scalar action. Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is
671	  stated against exactly this restriction-of-scalars / scalar-tower structure, and
672	  it is under that structure that localizing \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at
673	  \(a\) coincides with localizing \(M\) at \(\varphi a\).
674	
675	  % NOTE: This lemma is the affine case of the general fact that the pushforward of
676	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf along an affine morphism is quasi-coherent. In current
677	  % Mathlib master that fact is the IsLocalizing / fromTildeGamma characterization
678	  % (AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward); it post-dates the project's
679	  % pinned Mathlib, so the project proves it in-tree by the basic-open route above.
680	  % The in-tree proof remains valid and is not made redundant by a future Mathlib
681	  % bump.
682	  \medskip
683	  \emph{Quasi-coherence as a corollary.} The object isomorphism just constructed
684	  exhibits \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M} \cong
685	  \widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) as the tilde of a module. Tilde
686	  modules are quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
687	  is closed under isomorphism, so the pushforward
688	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. This
689	  conclusion is stated \emph{after} the object isomorphism, of which it is a
690	  consequence — no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' input is
691	  needed for the affine lane.
692	\end{proof}
693	
694	\begin{remark}
695	  This single isomorphism discharges both inputs that the affine reduction below
696	  requires of the pushforward of a tilde-module.
697	  \begin{enumerate}
698	    \item \emph{The global-sections comparison.} Applying the global-sections
699	      (module-of-sections) functor to the isomorphism identifies the
700	      \(R\)-module of sections of
701	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) with
702	      \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\); this is exactly the \(\Gamma\)-fragment
703	      comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map.
704	    \item \emph{Quasi-coherence of the pushforward.} The tilde of any module is
705	      quasi-coherent, and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules is
706	      closed under isomorphism; hence
707	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent.
708	      In particular no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' theorem is
709	      needed for the affine lane.
710	  \end{enumerate}
711	  Because both inputs the affine reduction asks of the pushforward are corollaries
712	  of this single object isomorphism — the section-level module identification and
713	  the quasi-coherence of the pushforward — this lemma is the sole bespoke
714	  ingredient the affine lane requires.
715	\end{remark}

717	\subsection{The pullback companion}
718	
719	The affine reduction below requires, alongside the pushforward dictionary, the
720	\emph{pullback} companion: the affine description of the inverse image of a
721	tilde-module. It is the affine case of the classical fact that the pullback of a
722	quasi-coherent sheaf is computed on modules by base change of scalars.
723	
724	The pullback companion is built by uniqueness of left adjoints from the
725	per-object \(\Gamma\)-fragment comparison of
726	Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, packaged as a natural isomorphism of
727	right-adjoint functors (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}).
728	
729	\begin{lemma}

732	[Naturality packaging of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison]
733	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
734	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardNatIso}
735	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
736	  The per-object comparison of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} assembles into a
737	  natural isomorphism of the two right-adjoint functors
738	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)}\) and
739	  \(\widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) on modules, exhibiting the
740	  pushforward dictionary as a natural transformation rather than merely a family of
741	  object isomorphisms. This is the input to the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints argument
742	  that produces the pullback companion Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}.
743	\end{lemma}

745	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
746	  The components are the object isomorphisms of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso};
747	  naturality is the corresponding naturality square, which holds because every
748	  constituent of the comparison is identity-on-elements.
749	\end{proof}

1135	/-- **Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback–pushforward unit.** For composable scheme morphisms
1136	`a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`, `b : X₂ ⟶ X₃` and a module `N` on `X₁`, the unit of the

1140	(`conjugateEquiv … (pullbackComp a b).inv = (pushforwardComp a b).hom`). It is the leg-reindex

1142	`(pullback.fst)`-unit into the affine `(Spec ι_A)`-unit modulo the transparent coherences.

1144	theorem pullbackPushforward_unit_comp {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃)
1145	    (N : X₃.Modules) :
1146	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1147	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1148	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1149	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1150	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app _
1151	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫
1152	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1153	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) := by
1154	  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv
1155	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).comp
1156	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a))
1157	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b))
1158	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv N
1159	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at h
1160	  rw [← Category.assoc]
1161	  exact h

1618	\begin{lemma}
1619	[(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence]
1620	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
1621	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
1622	  \uses{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1623	  Let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the canonical
1624	  isomorphism, \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the left tensor inclusion, and set
1625	  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\). For an \(A\)-module \(M\), the bare \((g')\)-unit
1626	  \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\) of the \((g')\)-adjunction expands as the four-factor composite
1627	  \[
1628	    \eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}
1629	      \;=\;
1630	      \eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}
1631	      \,\circ\, (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\bigl(\eta^{e}_{(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^{*}\widetilde M}\bigr)
1632	      \,\circ\, (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}
1633	      \,\circ\, (g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}\bigr),
1634	  \]
1635	  where composites are read left to right.
1636	\end{lemma}

1639	  The leg-reindex engine Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
1640	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), reads
1641	  \[
1642	    \eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}
1643	      \circ (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}(\eta^{e})
1644	      \circ (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}
1645	    \;=\;
1646	    \eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}
1647	      \circ (g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{inv}}\bigr).
1648	  \]
1649	  Post-compose both sides with \((g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\bigr)\).

1652	  to the identity, so the right-hand side collapses to \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\), giving the stated
1653	  expansion.

/-! ### Seam 2, step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, cut into five atomic links

The five lemmas `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand, gammaDistribute, eCancel,
affineUnit, innerMatch}` decompose the step-(iii) obligation of
`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs` into one mathematical move each. See blueprint
`lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand,…}`. -/

/-- **(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence.** For composable `a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`,
`b : X₂ ⟶ X₃`, a module `N` on `X₃`, and `g' = a ≫ b`, the bare `(g')`-unit expands as the
four-factor composite `η^b ≫ b_*(η^a) ≫ pushforwardComp(a,b).hom ≫ g'_*(pullbackComp(a,b).hom)`.
This is the inversion of the leg-reindex engine `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`: post-composing its
identity with `g'_*(pullbackComp.hom)` collapses the trailing `pullbackComp.inv` (functoriality of
`g'_*` on `inv ≫ hom = id`). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`. -/
theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules) (g' : X₁ ⟶ X₃) (hg' : g' = a ≫ b) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app N
      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app N) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward g').map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N) := by
  subst hg'
  rw [pullbackPushforward_unit_comp a b N, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp,
    Iso.inv_hom_id_app, Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]

theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
    {D : Type*} [Category D] (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules)
    (F : X₃.Modules ⥤ D) :
    F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N)
      = F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N) ≫
        F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map

            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
        F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean — fine-grained pass (iter-019)

Objective (PROGRESS.md): STUCK corrective — decompose the step-(iii) mate-unwinding crux of
`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs` into 5 standalone atomic lemmas (whole-goal attempts
PROHIBITED), prove bottom-up. Partial-success bar: `_unitExpand` + `_gammaDistribute` + `_eCancel`.

## Per-sentence log

### (iii-1) "the bare g'-unit expands as the 4-factor composite" → `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — proven sorry-free, validated under `lake build`.
- Stated abstractly: for composable `a,b` and module `N`,
  `η^{a≫b}_N = (η^b ≫ b_*(η^a) ≫ pushforwardComp(a,b).hom) ≫ (a≫b)_*(pullbackComp(a,b).hom)`.
  (Phrased with `a ≫ b` directly rather than a free `g'`+`hg'`, because a free `g'` makes the
  statement ill-typed before substitution — `pushforward g' ≠ pushforward (a≫b)` syntactically.)
- Proof: `rw [pullbackPushforward_unit_comp a b N]` (inverts the leg-reindex engine), then the
  trailing `(a≫b)_*(pullbackComp.inv ≫ pullbackComp.hom)` cancels. The cancellation `rw`s misfire on
  a **spurious `X.Modules` instance diamond** under single-file elaboration; closed instead in TERM
  MODE (`congrArg` + `Category.assoc` as a term + `Category.comp_id`), which checks defeq and tolerates
  the diamond. This was the "invert `key`" move the blueprint expected to "land quickly".

### (iii-2) "applying (Spec φ)_* and Γ to the expanded unit distributes over the four factors" → `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — proven sorry-free, validated under `lake build`.
- Stated abstractly: for any functor `F : X₃.Modules ⥤ D`, `F.map (η^{a≫b}) = (F u₁ ≫ F u₂ ≫ F u₃) ≫ F u₄`
  (the `unitExpand` factors). In the assembly `F = (Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R`, giving the four Γ-image factors.
- Proof: `rw [unitExpand]` then `F.map_comp` applied in TERM MODE (`(F.map_comp _ _).trans (congrArg …)`)
  — `rw/simp [Functor.map_comp]` is diamond-blocked on the `F.map (?f ≫ ?g)` pattern, term-mode unifies
  instances up to defeq.

### (iii-3) "the two e-factors + step-(ii) coherence cancel against the codomain read" → `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel`
- **Result:** NOT STATED (assembly-internal). Genuine blocker, see below.

### (iii-4) "the surviving affine unit equals restr_φ(η_M)" → `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit`
- **Result:** NOT STATED (assembly-internal). Seam 1 `base_change_mate_unit_value` (proven) +
  `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (proven) are the ingredients, but the lemma's type mentions the
  "affine codomain remnant Θ_tgt^aff" which only materialises mid-assembly.

### (iii-5) "read over Spec R by restr_ψ + ring-equation transport lands on ρ" → `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_innerMatch`
- **Result:** NOT STATED (assembly-internal). Definitional unfolding of `base_change_mate_inner_value`,
  but needs `affineUnit`'s output object to be the source of the statement.

### Assembly `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`
- Unchanged — still carries its `sorry`. I attempted to wire `unitExpand`/`gammaDistribute` into the
  goal at the sorry (after `subst hfst; subst hsnd`). **Both `rw`s fail to MATCH** the pattern
  `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (?a ≫ ?b)).unit.app ?N` against the goal's unit, even though the leg
  is literally `(pullbackSpecIso ↑R ↑A ↑R').hom ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom …)`: the post-`subst`
  **leg-lock** (documented in the file since iter-018) prevents the metavariable composite `?a ≫ ?b`
  from unifying with the locked leg `≫`. Updated the in-file note above the sorry to record this and
  the route forward (a `g'`-parametrised unit rewrite applied BEFORE `subst`).

## Summary
- **2 / 5 sub-lemmas closed** (`_unitExpand`, `_gammaDistribute`) — exactly the first two links of the
  bottleneck chain, both proven sorry-free and confirmed under `lake build`. Both are abstract/reusable
  (general schemes / general functor), not tied to the locked assembly.
- sorry count in file: **unchanged** (3 → 3: assembly `…_legs` @1490, Seam 3 @1667, FBC-B @1707). The 2
  new lemmas add ZERO sorries. No regressions.
- 3 / 5 remaining (`_eCancel`, `_affineUnit`, `_innerMatch`) NOT stated: they are assembly-internal —
  their precise Lean types are fixed by the goal state reached AFTER `gammaDistribute` + the
  codomain-read expansion, which the leg-lock currently prevents reaching cleanly.

## Blueprint markers ready
- `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand` → ready for `\leanok` (proof closed).
- `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute` → ready for `\leanok` (proof closed).
- (sync_leanok will set these deterministically.)

## Why I stopped — Partial progress
- **Real progress:** 2/5 sentences closed (`_unitExpand`, `_gammaDistribute`), sorry count 3→3 (the new
  lemmas are sorry-free additions). Both validated under `lake build`.
- **Key finding (for the planner):** the pervasive obstacle this pass was a **spurious `X.Modules`
  instance diamond** under single-file/LSP elaboration — `rw [Category.assoc]`, `rw/simp [Functor.map_comp]`,
  `rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]`, and even `rw [hI]` with a freshly-elaborated identical-looking `hI` all
  FAIL to match, while the SAME steps succeed in TERM MODE (`congrArg`, `.trans`, `Category.assoc`/`F.map_comp`
  as terms, `exact`) because elaboration unifies the instances up to defeq. Recommend the next pass on
  this file (and the eCancel telescoping) prefer term-mode combinators over `rw`/`simp` for any
  composition/functoriality/iso-cancellation step. (cf. memory `lake-build-vs-env-lean-spurious-errors`.)
- **The assembly crux (`_eCancel` + leg-lock) is the genuine remaining work**, unchanged in difficulty.
  The leg-lock blocks even *matching* `unitExpand` into the assembly. Recommended next directive:
  (1) refactor the assembly to rewrite the `(g')`-unit via a `g'`-parametrised lemma BEFORE `subst`
  (so `g'` is still a free local and matches syntactically), THEN (2) state+prove `_eCancel` inline
  against the resulting goal where its type is determined, then `_affineUnit` (Seam 1) and `_innerMatch`
  (definitional). All three should be authored INSIDE the assembly proof, not standalone.

content: File created successfully at: /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/.archon/task_results/FlatBaseChange.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

I've completed this fine-grained pass. Here's a summary of what I accomplished:

## Results

**2 of the 5 atomic step-(iii) sub-lemmas proven sorry-free** (validated under `lake build`):

1. **`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`** (iii-1) — the "invert `key`" move: expresses the bare `(g')`-unit as the four-factor composite. Proven by inverting `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`.

2. **`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`** (iii-2) — distributes the expansion through any functor (`(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ` in the assembly). Pure functoriality.

These are exactly the first two links of the bottleneck chain, both stated abstractly (reusable, not tied to the locked assembly). **No new sorries; no regressions** (file sorry count unchanged at 3: the assembly + 2 out-of-scope decls).

## Key finding for the planner

The dominant obstacle was a **spurious `X.Modules` instance diamond** under single-file/LSP elaboration: `rw [Category.assoc]`, `rw/simp [Functor.map_comp]`, `rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]`, and even `rw [hI]` with a freshly-elaborated identical hypothesis all *fail to match*, while the same steps succeed in **term mode** (`congrArg`, `.trans`, `Category.assoc`/`F.map_comp` as terms, `exact`) because elaboration unifies instances up to defeq. Both proofs use this term-mode workaround.

## What remains

- `_eCancel`, `_affineUnit`, `_innerMatch` (iii-3/4/5) were **not stated** — they are assembly-internal; their precise Lean types only materialize mid-assembly after `gammaDistribute` + the codomain-read expansion.
- The **leg-lock** (documented since iter-018) blocks even *matching* `unitExpand`/`gammaDistribute` into the assembly after `subst`. I updated the in-file note above the assembly's `sorry` recording this and the route forward: rewrite the `(g')`-unit via a `g'`-parametrised lemma *before* `subst`, then author eCancel/affineUnit/innerMatch inline. Recommended that the next pass prefer term-mode combinators over `rw`/`simp` throughout.

Full per-sentence log written to `.archon/task_results/FlatBaseChange.md`.

content: I'll start by reading the required context files.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Know your target in the dependency graph (leandag)
18	
19	The blueprint dependency graph is queryable **read-only** — `archon` is on PATH, `--json` is parseable (the banner goes to stderr). Use it to ground your work instead of guessing:
20	
21	- `archon dag-query node --node <label> --json` — your target's blueprint entry, its `\lean{}` pin, and status. **If the `\lean{}` pin is a `…TODO.…` placeholder, the Lean declaration does not exist yet** — you must *create* it (the typed `sorry`, or the proof if your mode builds it), not "fill" a name that isn't in the environment.
22	- `archon dag-query ancestors --node <label> --json` — your target's full dependency closure. Build on deps that are actually done (`\leanok`); a dependency still marked ∞ / `sorry` is **not** a sound foundation — cite it only if it genuinely typechecks today.
23	- `archon dag-query gaps --json` — the ∞ holes (statements with no informal proof). If your target's cone hits one, the blueprint sketch is incomplete: flag it in your task_result rather than inventing a proof to paper over it.
24	- `archon dag-query unmatched --json` — Lean decls with no blueprint entry. After you add helpers, your new names surface here until they're blueprinted — that is the debt you must report (see Logging → "Needs blueprint entry").
25	
26	The label↔Lean mapping is the `\lean{}` annotation in the blueprint block; `leandag stats` / `leandag focus` also give a project-wide view. You never *write* the graph — that's the dag/plan/review agents' job — but reading it keeps you from proving against a hollow or unfinished foundation.
27	
28	## Protected declarations
29	
30	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
31	
32	## Talking to the user (TO_USER.md)
33	
34	`.archon/TO_USER.md` is a *persistent* shared notice board surfaced to the user as a banner. You may add a bullet **only** for something the user genuinely must act on that you hit while proving — e.g. a missing credential/dependency a sorry is gated on, or a `/- USER: -/` question you cannot resolve. Discipline:
35	
36	- **Concise + relevant**: keep the whole file ≤ 2–3 short bullets; before adding, read it and delete any bullet no longer true. Never a question queue — the loop never waits.
37	- **Concurrency**: provers run in parallel (one per file). To avoid clobbering a sibling lane's bullet, append your single bullet prefixed with your file (`- [Foo/Bar.lean] …`) rather than rewriting the whole file, and only when it's truly user-actionable. Routine "couldn't close this sorry" notes go in `task_results/<your_file>.md`, NOT here.
38	
39	## Avoid early termination
40	
41	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
42	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
43	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
44	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
45	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
46	
47	## Task completion criteria
48	
49	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
50	
51	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
52	2. Zero axioms introduced.
53	3. The file compiles cleanly.
54	
55	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
56	
57	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
58	
59	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
60	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
61	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
62	
63	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
64	
65	## When infrastructure is missing
66	
67	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
68	
69	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
70	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
71	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
72	
73	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
74	
75	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
76	
77	## Proof style
78	
79	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
80	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
81	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
82	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
83	- **List every new declaration you introduce under a `## Needs blueprint entry` heading in your task_result** (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) — see Logging below. The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: the review agent and planner consume this list (and `archon dag-query unmatched`) to give each new declaration a blueprint block. An unreported helper is an invisible, isolated dependency that silently corrupts the frontier — flagging it is mandatory, not optional. (You never write the blueprint yourself; you only name what needs one.)
84	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
85	
86	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
87	
88	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
89	
90	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
91	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
92	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
93	
94	## LSP MCP tools
95	
96	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
97	
98	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
99	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
100	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
101	
102	## Search protocol
103	
104	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
105	
106	1. `lean_local_search` first.
107	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
108	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
109	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
110	
111	## Tooling traps
112	
113	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
114	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
115	
116	## Logging
117	
118	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
119	
120	```markdown
121	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
122

124	### Attempt 1
125	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
126	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
127	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
128	
129	### Attempt 2
130	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
131	- **Result:** RESOLVED
132	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
133	
134	## helper_bijective (line 78)
135	### Attempt 1
136	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
137	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
138	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
139	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
140	
141	## Needs blueprint entry
142	- `Algebra.WLocal.helper_bijective` (line 78) — new helper, no blueprint block yet. Uses: `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective`. Reviewer/planner: add a `\label` + `\lean` + `\uses` entry so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
143	```
144	
145	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter. **Always include the `## Needs blueprint entry` section when you added any new declaration** (omit it only if you added none) — it is how the loop keeps Lean ↔ blueprint at 1-to-1.
146	
147	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
148

150

152

154	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

39	## Decisions identified
40	
41	### Decision 1: how to express the generic rank `m`
42	
43	- **Mathlib idiom**: `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)`.
44	  `P_d = MvPolynomial (Fin d) A` is a noetherian domain (`MvPolynomial.isDomain`, Hilbert basis), so
45	  `nonZeroDivisors P_d` localizes `N` to a module over `Localization (nonZeroDivisors P_d) = FractionRing P_d`,
46	  a finite-dimensional `FractionRing P_d`-vector space (finite, since `N` is finite over `P_d`).
47	  `Module.finrank` over a field is the right invariant; `Module.rank` (`Cardinal`-valued) is the wrong
48	  altitude — you want a `ℕ` to index `Fin m`.
49	- **Choosing `m` lifts of `N` whose images form a basis** — atoms all verified:
50	  - `Module.finBasis (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule … N) : Basis (Fin m) (FractionRing P_d) (…)`
51	    (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Free`) — the field-vector-space basis indexed by `Fin (finrank)`.
52	  - `IsLocalizedModule.surj (nonZeroDivisors P_d) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap …)` — every basis vector
53	    is `(1/s) • (image of some n ∈ N)`; `s` a unit in the field, so the **images** `{mk n_i}` are again a
54	    basis (clear denominators). (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`.)
55	  - `LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars` (P_d ↪ FractionRing P_d injective) descends linear independence
56	    of the lifts from `FractionRing P_d` back to `P_d` — this is what makes `φ` (below) injective.
57	  - Reverse helper `Module.Basis.ofIsLocalizedModule` / `LinearIndependent.localization`
58	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) go R→Rₛ; useful for the spanning/surjectivity half.
59	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent — Mathlib has every atom but **no single packaged "choose `m` elements
60	  whose images are a basis of the localization" lemma**. The number is idiomatic; the lift is project glue.
61	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the number (`Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule …)`),
62	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL on the lift (small, all anchors present).
63	
64	### Decision 2: encoding the generic-rank SES `gf_generic_rank_ses`
65	
66	- **Mathlib idiom for the map**: `φ := Fintype.linearCombination P_d v` where `v : Fin m → N` are the
67	  lifts of Decision 1 (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Finsupp.LinearCombination`). Domain `(Fin m → P_d)` **is**
68	  the concrete `P_d^{⊕m}` (matches the `M'` slot of L3 exactly). Companions: `Fintype.linearCombination_apply`
69	  (`∑ i, f i • v i`), `Fintype.range_linearCombination` (`range = span (Set.range v)`).
70	  `φ` injective ⟺ `LinearIndependent P_d v` (got from Decision 1 via `restrict_scalars`).
71	- **Cokernel / torsion**: `T := N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ`; the surjection is `Submodule.mkQ (range φ)`
72	  (surjective, and `Function.Exact φ (mkQ (range φ))` holds — `range φ = ker (mkQ)`). `T` torsion encoded
73	  as `Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T` (preferred — it is the literal input of the reindex's
74	  annihilator anchor) or equivalently `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) T)` via
75	  `LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff`. Torsion holds because the images of `v` span the localization
76	  (basis), so `φ ⊗ FractionRing P_d` is surjective, hence `T ⊗ FractionRing P_d = 0`.
77	- **Important simplification vs the directive**: the SES is constructible over `P_d` **directly — no
78	  inversion of `g ∈ A` is needed for this step**. `φ` is injective on the nose (`ker φ` is torsion in the
79	  torsion-free `P_d`-module `P_d^{⊕m}`, hence `0`), and `T` is torsion on the nose. The `g` that Nitsure
80	  mentions belongs entirely to the *reindex* (Decision 3), not the SES. So `gf_generic_rank_ses` carries
81	  no `g`. (The "denominator-clearing exactness after inverting one element" API the directive asked about
82	  is therefore unnecessary here.)
83	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — no packaged generic-rank SES, but every atom exists and the assembly
84	  is short.
85	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (build `gf_generic_rank_ses` from the atoms above).
86	
87	### Decision 3: the torsion reindex `gf_torsion_reindex` — and the base-domain induction shape
88	
89	- **Nonzero annihilator (Mathlib ✓)**: `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
90	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Torsion.Basic`): a finite `Module.IsTorsion R M` over comm `R` has
91	  `(⊤.annihilator ∩ nonZeroDivisors R).Nonempty`. For the domain `P_d` this gives `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`.
92	- **Variable drop (Mathlib-ABSENT)**: from `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)`, one wants `T` module-finite over a polynomial
93	  ring in `m' < d` variables. Cleanest route that **avoids Krull-dimension theory**: a Nagata change of
94	  variables makes `F` monic in `X_d` up to its leading coefficient `g ∈ A`; after inverting `g`,
95	  `A_g[X_1..X_d]/(F)` is module-finite (division algorithm) over `A_g[X_1..X_{d-1}] = MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) A_g`,
96	  and `T` (finite over `P_d/(F)`) is finite over it by `Module.Finite.trans`. So `m' = d-1 < d` — **no
97	  `ringKrullDim` needed**. Mathlib has the *field* Noether normalization `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`
98	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) and `ringKrullDim` theory (`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.*`,
99	  e.g. `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_le`) but **not** the domain-level single-variable elimination with
100	  denominator clearing. This is the **same engine** the already-stubbed L4
101	  `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial` needs — build once, reuse.
102	- **CRITICAL design consequence — the induction must generalize the base domain `A`.** The reindex inverts
103	  `g ∈ A`, so `T_g` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g)` — base ring `A_g`, **not**
104	  `A`. The IH of `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` as currently written fixes `A`, so
105	  `IH m' _ T_g` does **not typecheck** (wrong base ring). This is the real cause of the iter-006
106	  "signatures depend on the generic-rank API" stall: it is not the rank API, it is that the recursion
107	  changes the base. **Fix**: state/prove the core with the motive universally quantifying the noetherian
108	  domain — `∀ d, ∀ (A) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (N) […], ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Free A_f N_f`,
109	  by `Nat.strong_induction_on d` with `A` (and its three instances, and all of `N`'s `d`-dependent
110	  instances) reverted into the motive. `A` is already an explicit argument, so this is a reversion change,
111	  not a signature change to the public lemma. Apply `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) (Localization.Away g) T_g` at
112	  base `A_g` (still a noetherian domain: `IsLocalization.isDomain`/`Localization.Away` of a noeth domain).
113	- **Descent of the IH witness back to `A`**: IH gives `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `T_g` free over `(A_g)_h`.
114	  Since `A_g = Localization.Away g` and `h = a / g^k`, `(A_g)_h ≅ A_{g·a}` and `T_{g·a} ≅ (T_g)_h`, so the
115	  freeness descends to `f'' := g·a ∈ A`. This is the same localization-tower freeness transport as the
116	  already-proved L3b `free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul` (a thin bridge across `A → A_g → (A_g)_h`).
117	- **The `P_d^{⊕m}` end is free over `A_f` for free (Mathlib ✓)**: `MvPolynomial.instFree`
118	  (`Module.Free A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)`) + `Pi`/localization free, so the `M'` end of L3 is free over
119	  `A_{f'}` for *any* `f' ≠ 0`. (Note: the file's comment "applied coordinatewise via the d=0 leaf" is
120	  slightly off — `P_d^{⊕m}` is **not** module-finite over `A`, but it **is** free, which is all L3 needs.)
121	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (variable-drop engine + base-`A` generalization).
122	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.

124	## Recommended sub-lemma signatures (for the blueprint pin)
125	
126	```lean
127	/-- `gf_generic_rank_ses` — the generic-rank short exact sequence. No `g`: built over `P_d` directly. -/
128	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
129	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
130	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
131	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
132	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
133	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
134	      Function.Injective φ ∧
135	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)
136	-- witness: m = finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N);
137	--          φ = Fintype.linearCombination P_d v, v the denominator-cleared basis lifts.
138	```
139	
140	```lean
141	/-- `gf_torsion_reindex` — re-present a finite torsion `P_d`-module over fewer variables after
142	inverting `g ∈ A`. Mirrors the existential style of the already-present L4
143	`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`. -/
144	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
145	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
146	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
147	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
148	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
149	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
150	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
151	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
152	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
153	      (_ : Module (Localization.Away g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
154	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
155	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
156	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
157	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
158	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)
159	-- can fix m' = d - 1 (single-variable elimination); strong induction accepts any m' < d.
160	```
161	
162	And the **core's proof must be restructured** to generalize `A`:
163	```lean
164	-- helper, then `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` is its specialization:
165	∀ d, ∀ (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
166	       (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
167	       [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module A N]
168	       [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N],
169	     ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N)
170	-- proved by `Nat.strong_induction_on d`, A reverted into the motive.
171	```
172	
173	## Recommendation
174	Pin `m := Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d) (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N)` as the generic
175	rank — idiomatic and `ℕ`-valued. Build `gf_generic_rank_ses` (no `g`) from `Fintype.linearCombination`
176	on denominator-cleared basis lifts (`Module.finBasis` + `IsLocalizedModule.surj` +
177	`LinearIndependent.restrict_scalars`), with `T := N ⧸ range φ` and `Module.IsTorsion P_d T`. Build
178	`gf_torsion_reindex` on the L4 existential template, using `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`
179	for `0 ≠ F ∈ Ann(T)` and the **project-side** Nagata single-variable elimination + denominator clearing
180	(shared with L4) to land over `MvPolynomial (Fin (d-1)) (Localization.Away g)`. **The load-bearing fix is
181	structural**: generalize the base domain `A` in the `Nat.strong_induction_on`, because the reindex changes
182	the base ring to `A_g` and the IH must be available there. None of these require weakening any pinned type.
